mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in retail sales indicates robust consumer demand, which translates to higher corporate earnings and thus positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days. This reflects a healthy consumption-driven economy.
failure_condition: If the 20-day forward returns are negative more than 50% of the time when RSAFS shows positive month-over-month growth, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Extremely low federal funds rates, sustained near the zero lower bound, signal an accommodative monetary policy environment that encourages risk-taking and boosts equity valuations by compressing discount rates, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative or flat (within +/- 0.5%) in more than 40% of periods where FEDFUNDS is below 0.25%, this hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of high unemployment and declining payrolls signals a weakening labor market, which reduces consumer spending and corporate profits, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as economic outlook deteriorates.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE > 7.0% AND PAYEMS declines for 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: If S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 30% of periods where UNRATE is above 7.0% and PAYEMS has declined for two consecutive months, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in industrial production indicates a healthy manufacturing sector and overall economic expansion, which supports corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a short 5-day horizon, reflecting immediate market optimism.
failure_condition: If the 5-day forward returns are negative more than 55% of the time when INDPRO shows positive month-over-month growth, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A significant rebound in housing starts from depressed levels, especially during a post-recession recovery, signals renewed confidence and investment in the economy. This acts as a leading indicator for broader economic growth and positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: If, during periods identified as post-recession recovery, a >10% MoM increase in HOUST is followed by negative 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 35% of instances, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: High inflation combined with low federal funds rates indicates a negative real interest rate environment. This typically leads to investors seeking inflation hedges in equities, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon as capital flows into risk assets.
regime_hint: inflationary environment with accommodative monetary policy
failure_condition: If S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 40% of periods where CPIAUCSL annual growth exceeds 3.0% and FEDFUNDS is below 1.0%, this hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A significant decrease in the unemployment rate signals improving labor market conditions, boosting consumer confidence and spending. This ultimately supports corporate earnings and leads to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon due to immediate market reaction.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE declines by > 0.2 percentage points month-over-month
failure_condition: If a month-over-month decline in UNRATE of more than 0.2 percentage points is followed by negative 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 45% of instances, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth, even if moderate, reflects an expanding economy that provides a fundamental tailwind for corporate revenues and profits. This leads to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon as investors price in future growth.
regime_hint: any regime with positive GDP growth
failure_condition: If the 60-day forward returns are negative more than 50% of the time when GDPC1 shows positive quarter-over-quarter growth, the hypothesis is rejected.
